title: Pseudohemipilia kabompoensis
taxonomy: Pseudohemipilia kabompoensis is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Zambia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is a type of epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants. Pseudohemipilia kabompoensis has been classified as Endangered on the IUCN Red List due to its limited geographic range and threatened habitat. Its classification as a species within the Orchidaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchids.

intro: Pseudohemipilia kabompoensis, also known as Kabompo orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Zambia. The Kabompo orchid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudohemipilia kabompoensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
